ogTitle: Framlingham East vs Franklinford: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Framlingham East and Franklinford.
heading: Framlingham East vs Franklinford — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Framlingham East, Victoria and Franklinford, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Franklinford has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Framlingham East (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Framlingham East or Franklinford safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Framlingham East has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Franklinford scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Framlingham East scores 65/100 for bushfire risk. Franklinford scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Framlingham East or Franklinford?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Franklinford has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
